Summary: FC-01 Define ICP tiers and qualification rubric (US executives + coaches)
Description:
Objective:
Create a strict ICP and qualification framework to prevent low-quality pipeline and shorten close cycles.

Scope:
- Define segment tiers for individual executives and coach buyers.
- Define disqualification rules (timing, authority, budget, fit).
- Add qualification fields to outbound tracker.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Approved ICP matrix exists and is used by both founder and Mauricio.
- 100% of outbound records include ICP tier + qualification status.
- Disqualification reason is mandatory for all rejected leads.

Business Impact:
Directly improves acquisition efficiency and founder time allocation.

Summary: FC-02 Finalize offer architecture and pricing narrative
Description:
Objective:
Package offers for fast close while preserving expansion path.

Scope:
- Individual executive offer at $199/month.
- Coach seat model at 25% discount vs individual plan.
- One-page pricing and value framing for outreach and calls.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Offer docs approved and published for internal use.
- All outbound and call scripts use a consistent pricing narrative.
- Objection handling includes budget and ROI response patterns.

Business Impact:
Improves close rate and reduces confusion in sales conversations.

Summary: FC-03 Build messaging matrix for LinkedIn and email
Description:
Objective:
Create segment-specific messaging that increases positive replies and meeting quality.

Scope:
- 3 message angles per segment.
- Subject line library and CTA variants.
- Objection response library tied to pain points.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Messaging matrix v1 is documented and versioned.
- At least 2 A/B variants are launched per channel.
- Reply coding taxonomy is defined (positive, neutral, reject, no-fit).

Business Impact:
Primary lever for top-of-funnel conversion.

Summary: FC-04 Build first 150-target outbound list with QA
Description:
Objective:
Stand up a clean target list for week-2 execution.

Scope:
- 150 US contacts across executive and coach segments.
- Include title, company, trigger context, and outreach channel.
- QA for duplicates and stale records.

Acceptance Criteria:
- At least 150 contacts loaded with >= 90% field completeness.
- Duplicate rate below 3%.
- Segment split is documented and approved.

Business Impact:
Improves reach quality and outbound efficiency.

Summary: FC-05 Launch week-2 outbound wave with daily logging
Description:
Objective:
Execute first controlled outbound wave and build evidence baseline.

Scope:
- Run LinkedIn + email cadence daily.
- Log all touches and outcomes in single tracker.
- Daily checkpoint with founder.

Acceptance Criteria:
- >= 120 touches completed by end of week.
- Daily updates delivered with blockers and next actions.
- Positive reply baseline captured by channel and segment.

Business Impact:
Converts planning into measurable acquisition motion.

Summary: FC-06 Implement 24-hour warm-lead founder handoff SLA
Description:
Objective:
Reduce lead decay and increase booked-call conversion.

Scope:
- Define handoff template (context, pain, urgency, ask).
- Define owner and fallback if founder unavailable.
- Track response latency and outcome.

Acceptance Criteria:
- >= 90% warm leads receive response within 24 hours.
- Handoff template used on 100% of warm leads.
- Weekly SLA report shows misses and root causes.

Business Impact:
Increases speed-to-close and protects early trust.

Summary: FC-07 Build proof asset pack for close support
Description:
Objective:
Equip calls and follow-ups with concise trust assets.

Scope:
- 3 case-style snippets (problem, action, outcome).
- FAQ items including credibility concerns and disclaimers.
- One-page proof summary for outbound follow-up.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Proof pack published and used in all stage-2 opportunities.
- Objection handling document reviewed weekly.
- Meeting-to-opportunity conversion improves versus baseline.

Business Impact:
Improves conversion quality without major engineering effort.

Summary: FC-08 Improve onboarding UX above-the-fold
Description:
Objective:
Increase activation by reducing first-session friction.

Scope:
- Move key onboarding inputs horizontal/above-the-fold where practical.
- Clarify first-value path and required fields.
- Reduce scroll depth and cognitive load in onboarding flow.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Updated onboarding UI shipped to production.
- Signup-to-first-value funnel instrumented.
- Activation rate improves by at least 20% from baseline in 30 days.

Business Impact:
Direct acquisition and retention lift from better first experience.

Summary: FC-09 Upgrade brief quality for executive, board, and interview contexts
Description:
Objective:
Increase perceived value and close confidence through better brief depth.

Scope:
- Add board/interviewer expectation section to briefs.
- Add role-specific guidance for executive interview strategy.
- Add quality checklist and review loop.

Acceptance Criteria:
- New brief sections shipped and visible in generated outputs.
- Brief quality rubric documented.
- User feedback on brief usefulness collected for every demo/pilot account.

Business Impact:
Improves product differentiation and conversion.

Summary: FC-10 Improve target company search and relevance
Description:
Objective:
Increase repeat usage and practical value in core workflow.

Scope:
- Improve target company search UX and ranking relevance.
- Reduce false-positive search outcomes.
- Add quick path to save top targets.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Search improvement shipped with measurable latency and relevance checks.
- User task-completion tests show faster target selection.
- Search success metric is tracked weekly.

Business Impact:
Supports acquisition demos and retention through daily utility.

Summary: FC-11 Implement action scoring across user journey
Description:
Objective:
Quantify which actions correlate to conversion and retention.

Scope:
- Define event taxonomy for key user actions.
- Assign action score weights.
- Capture score trend at user and cohort levels.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Event schema documented and implemented in analytics.
- Score calculation runs daily.
- Weekly report includes top and bottom action contributors.

Business Impact:
Turns product decisions into evidence-driven prioritization.

Summary: FC-12 Create top and bottom quartile action effectiveness report
Description:
Objective:
Operationalize product learning for weekly execution decisions.

Scope:
- Compute top 25% and bottom 25% actions by impact.
- Break out by segment (executive vs coach).
- Publish recurring report for growth review.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Weekly report auto-generated and distributed.
- Bottom quartile actions have explicit remove/fix decision each week.
- Top quartile actions have scale plan each week.

Business Impact:
Accelerates compound learning and removes low-value work quickly.

Summary: FC-13 Build Mauricio execution page with tasks and rationale
Description:
Objective:
Provide one source of truth for Mauricio sprint tasks and why each task matters.

Scope:
- Create public noindex execution page.
- Include weekly tasks, outputs, metrics, and owners.
- Link from kickoff page.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Page is live and reviewed by founder + Mauricio.
- Weekly updates are reflected in page content.
- Day-30 gates clearly documented.

Business Impact:
Improves accountability and execution clarity during trial period.

Summary: FC-14 Create BD partner intake page with lead capture
Description:
Objective:
Convert coach and B2B interest into structured pilot opportunities.

Scope:
- Build page with pilot value proposition and intake form.
- Route form data to sales tracker.
- Include qualification checklist for follow-up.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Intake page is live and connected to workflow.
- New submissions trigger owner assignment within 1 business day.
- Submission-to-first-response SLA is tracked.

Business Impact:
Supports 3-pilot target and partner channel growth.

Summary: FC-15 Publish trust FAQ and site disclaimer
Description:
Objective:
Reduce trust friction at decision stage and improve conversion quality.

Scope:
- Add FAQ entry addressing credibility concerns.
- Add disclaimer: no guarantee of success.
- Ensure copy aligns with legal and positioning standards.

Acceptance Criteria:
- FAQ and disclaimer deployed on site.
- Linked from pricing and key decision pages.
- Sales team uses aligned language in calls and outreach.

Business Impact:
Improves trust while reducing legal/expectation risk.

Summary: FC-16 Add pre-prod UI/UX and AP-style release checks
Description:
Objective:
Raise output quality and reduce regressions before production release.

Scope:
- Define automated checks for UI/UX and AP style compliance.
- Add checks to CI and release workflow.
- Document pass/fail and override policy.

Acceptance Criteria:
- CI gate blocks merge on failed checks.
- Release checklist includes UI/UX and style status.
- False positive rate is tracked and tuned.

Business Impact:
Supports brand consistency and production quality at scale.

Summary: FC-17 Add PR-level error checking and logging standards
Description:
Objective:
Improve reliability and diagnosability as user volume scales.

Scope:
- Add PR checks for key error and logging requirements.
- Define minimum logging fields for critical flows.
- Add examples and lint-style enforcement where possible.

Acceptance Criteria:
- PR template/checklist includes logging requirements.
- Critical routes comply with logging contract.
- Weekly audit reports top logging gaps.

Business Impact:
Improves operational support readiness for 1,000 users.

Summary: FC-18 Day-30 trial decision memo and 60-90 scale plan
Description:
Objective:
Make an objective continue/expand/stop decision based on trial outcomes.

Scope:
- Compile scorecard performance against day-14/day-30 targets.
- Quantify channel economics and pipeline quality.
- Recommend next 60-day operating plan.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Decision memo approved within 2 business days after day 30.
- 60-90 day plan includes owner, milestones, and resource needs.
- Go/no-go criteria are explicit and archived.

Business Impact:
Prevents drift and preserves capital/time for highest-return work.

Summary: FC-19 Coach pilot playbook and cohort reporting package
Description:
Objective:
Package coach use case for repeatable B2B pilot conversion and expansion.

Scope:
- Define coach onboarding, seat setup, and reporting cadence.
- Create cohort metrics template and pilot scorecard.
- Build pilot close deck narrative.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Playbook and scorecard are used in all coach pilot conversations.
- At least 3 qualified pilot opportunities have standardized reporting scope.
- Post-pilot expansion path is documented.

Summary: FC-20 90-day closeout report and Jira backlog refresh
Description:
Objective:
Create final closeout with outcomes, misses, and next-quarter backlog.

Scope:
- Summarize revenue, pipeline, conversion, and product impact.
- Highlight top and bottom initiatives by measured results.
- Generate next-quarter prioritized Jira import pack.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Closeout report shared with leadership.
- Next-quarter Jira import file produced with acceptance criteria.
- Backlog reflects measured impact, not assumptions.
